steps.title: Keep the phrase attached
steps.content: The sentence says the manuscripts survived to the present day by being passed down through private libraries. The preposition by must attach directly to the gerund phrase being passed down, without punctuation interrupting that connection.

steps.title: Reject boundary breaks
steps.content: Choice B uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. Choice C turns that phrase into a sentence fragment. Choice D puts a comma between the preposition by and its object. The answer is A.
